sources.videoTitle: Abomasnow COMPLETELY Breaks Teams In GBL [Pokémon GO PvP]

performanceNotes: Abomasnow's buffed Powder Snow provides excellent fast move pressure
Quagsire's Stone Edge provides critical coverage against Fire and Flying types
Corviknight can farm down and sweep with Air Cutter boost potential
description: Shadow Abomasnow lead with Shadow Quagsire safe swap and Corviknight closer. Abomasnow's unique Ice/Grass typing provides strong neutral coverage with Powder Snow, Weather Ball, and Energy Ball, while Quagsire handles Electric and Fire threats and Corviknight rounds out the team.

performanceNotes: Ice Beam Jellicent is the secret sauce — provides unexpected ice coverage instead of standard Surf
Aegislash's shield/blade form mechanic allows it to tank hits and store back-to-back Shadow Balls
Used as a climbing team by a Legend-tier player
description: Shadow Hydreigon lead applies heavy fast-move and core-breaking pressure, with a double-ghost backline of Ice Beam Jellicent (safe swap) and Aegislash (closer) that walls fighters and steels.

sources.videoTitle: OPPRESSIVE Shadow Hydreigon Applies INSANE Pressure in the Great League!!

sources.videoTitle: I Reached EXPERT RANK With The MOST Underrated Pokémon In The Game [Pokémon GO PvP]
sources.author: ItsAXN

description: Grumpig-focused team that reached Expert rank. Altaria leads with Empoleon as safe swap and Grumpig closing. Grumpig's wide coverage with Psywave, Shadow Ball, and Dynamic Punch makes it a safe and versatile closer.
performanceNotes: Used to reach Expert rank this season
Grumpig's coverage with Shadow Ball and Dynamic Punch handles a wide range of threats
Altaria consistently puts in heavy work as the lead, farming down opponents

sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion
sources.videoTitle: LEGEND PUSHING WITH GRUMPIG!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

performanceNotes: Went 5-0 in a set right before recording, followed by a 9-1 run across three sets
Grumpig shines as a two-shield closer capable of beating threats like Gourgeist
Feraligatr's Shadow Claw + Hydro Cannon provides strong neutral pressure and shield bait
description: Altaria lead with Grumpig safe swap and Feraligatr closer. The strategy revolves around using Altaria's bulk in the lead to draw out anti-Psychic or Dark types, then leveraging Grumpig's hard-hitting charge moves to clean up in the back.

sources.videoTitle: What Happened?? My Orlando Regional Run!! | Pokémon GO PvP
sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion

performanceNotes: Won Game 1 with all three Pokémon still alive
Altaria walled Diggersby's energy while Stunfisk aligned favorably against Wigglytuff
description: Altaria lead with Feraligatr and Galarian Stunfisk in back. Used in Game 1 vs Hungry Wolfpack at the Orlando Regional, leveraging Altaria's safe lead profile to maintain switch advantage and align Stunfisk against Wigglytuff.

sources.videoTitle: SHADOW CAMERUPTION!! High Level Camerupt Plays!! | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion

description: Shadow Camerupt lead with Galarian Moltres safe swap and Lapras closer. ABB-style line that lures out Rock/Ground counters like Bastiodon, then punishes with banked energy and Rock Tomb debuffs.
performanceNotes: Camerupt's high attack with Incinerate + Rock Tomb/Scorching Sands hits hard and stacks debuffs
Strategy revolves around banking energy on Camerupt for late-game sweeps
Went undefeated across all shown sets, piloted by Worlds Top 8 finisher MaxiMillionP

sources.videoTitle: *Buffed* Fury Cutter Heracross has the UPPER HAND!! | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion

performanceNotes: Heracross generates energy fast with Fury Cutter for Upper Hand spam and Rock Tomb coverage against flyers
Charjabug with Volt Switch/Discharge acts as a closer and hard-counters Water/Flying types
Won multiple sets including comebacks from bad leads like Azumarill
description: Triple bug team featuring buffed Fury Cutter Heracross as the lead, with Golisopod and Charjabug providing coverage. Submitted by viewer Hungry Wolfpack for Spicy Saturday.

sources.videoTitle: WHY Is NO ONE Is Using This Pokémon?! [Pokémon GO PvP]
sources.author: ItsAXN

description: Galarian Corsola lead leveraging Astonish fast move pressure and Power Gem coverage, with Shadow Drapion safe swap and Corviknight closer. Built around Corsola's bulk to absorb hits and get shields down.
performanceNotes: Corsola's bulk compensates for slow energy generation, applying consistent fast move pressure
Won a tough game 1 against Mightyena/Wigglytuff by not giving up despite bad lead
Shadow Drapion provides strong neutral damage and energy-efficient Aqua Tail + Crunch coverage

description: Guzzlord lead with Politoed safe swap and Corviknight closer. Politoed provides spammy damage with Mud Shot plus Weather Ball/Ice Beam coverage, while Guzzlord and Corviknight form a solid backline core.
performanceNotes: Politoed's Weather Ball output surprisingly high — two-shots several opponents
Guzzlord + Corviknight core handles Dartrix and other common threats
Went positive in the set during ELO placement battles

performanceNotes: Performed very well at ~2450 ELO

Drapion as safe swap baits ground and water answers that Jellicent handles
description: Jellicent lead with Drapion safe swap to draw out ground types, which Jellicent can beat, freeing Shadow Typhlosion to sweep in the back with Blast Burn pressure.

sources.videoTitle: META BREAKING MOVESET! MILTANK has PERFECT COVERAGE for the Great League! Pokémon Go PvP

description: Medicham lead with Shadow Drapion safe swap and Miltank closer. Miltank's unique Steel + Electric coverage via Gyro Ball and Thunderbolt core-breaks common meta pairings like Altaria/Empoleon and Wigglytuff/Jellicent.
performanceNotes: Miltank's Gyro Ball + Thunderbolt coverage core-breaks Altaria/Empoleon and Wigglytuff/Jellicent

Went undefeated across all shown matches

sources.videoTitle: CHEAT CODE! I Hit VETERAN EARLY with *NEW* SCALD VOLCANINON in Pokemon GO

performanceNotes: Hit Veteran (2550 ELO) early in the season, battling world champs and regional winners
Volcanion dominates Clefairy, Marill, Amoonguss, Whimsicott, and Gourgeist
Weak to ground types (Clodsire) which became more common at higher ELO
description: Hisuian Electrode lead with Tentacruel safe swap and Volcanion closer. Volcanion's new Scald move combined with Overheat and Incinerate fast move makes it a dominant closer against the steel/grass/fairy-heavy meta.

performanceNotes: Gabite reaches Twisters very quickly with buffed Mud Shot (5-4 pacing)
Flamethrower provides coverage against Steel/Grass types like Ferrothorn (one-shots double SE)
Shadow Garchomp's Sand Tomb provides permanent debuffs to set up Gabite sweeps
description: ABB style team with Empoleon lead and double ground/dragon in the back. Gabite and Shadow Garchomp share similar typings, forcing out counters for Empoleon to handle.

performanceNotes: Went 3-0 against Togekiss leads by sacrificing Feraligatr to grab shields then cleaning with Medicham

Flexible safe swap usage: Feraligatr can be played as true safe swap or as a sacrifice play depending on the lead matchup
description: Lickilicky lead with Rollout fast move for Talonflame/flyer pressure, Shadow Feraligatr as the #1 ranked safe swap grabbing shields with Hydro Cannon spam, and Medicham closer with Ice Punch and Dynamic Punch coverage for flyers and grounds.

Bug Bite Forretress beats Quagsire cleanly, unlike Volt Switch which struggles in that matchup

performanceNotes: Shadow Gurdurr benefits from Low Kick buff, hits hard with Brick Break and Stone Edge for coverage
Shadow Golbat repeatedly steals the show, core-breaking opponents with Poison Fang pressure and Shadow Ball
Struggles against Jellicent which resists Sealeo's energy and walls the team
description: Shadow Gurdurr lead team leveraging the buffed Low Kick fast move, with Shadow Sealeo as safe swap and Shadow Golbat as closer. Submitted by viewer Jason for Spicy Saturday.
Shadow Galarian Weezing lead with Overheat as a personal preference, Shadow Quagsire safe swap benefiting from the Mud Shot buff, and Guzzlord closer with great typing and resistances.
